news.Title: Morgan Stanley delivers blunt message to Tesla stock investors
news.Content: Tesla’s ( TSLA ) recent drop has investors asking the tough questions, but Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco has a clear response. The bank reiterated an equal-weight rating on the stock while keeping its $415 price target , a 17% upside from current levels. Tesla stock, though, has been under duress of late after a major delivery miss in Q1 , rattling confidence, sparking concerns over demand and margins. On top of that, there’s a new wrinkle. Tesla’s energy storage business, which has performed consistently over the years, also disappointed. Coupled with a steep drop in deliveries, it was enough ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Morgan Stanley reiterated an Equal-Weight rating on Tesla (TSLA) with a $415 price target, implying 17% upside.
• Tesla's Q1 delivery miss and disappointment in the energy storage segment have rattled investor confidence.
• Energy storage deployments fell 15.4% year-over-year, marking the first decline since 2022.
• Morgan Stanley views the energy storage miss as a delay rather than a trend, citing improving economics and demand from data centers.
• The firm believes Tesla's valuation hinges on its ability to scale an unsupervised Robotaxi fleet, with Austin and seven new city launches as key signals.
news.Keywords: Tesla, TSLA, Morgan Stanley, analyst rating, deliveries, energy storage, robotaxi, AI, valuation

news.Title: These Growth Stocks Surged 300% and 1,400% While Everyone Was Watching Nvidia, and They Are Still Cheap. Here's Why.
news.Content: While Nvidia remains the poster child for the artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure buildout, it has been far from the best-performing stock in the space over the past year. While Nvidia's stock is up a strong 60% over this period, Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) has a 300% gain, and Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) is up nearly 1,400%. Micron and Sandisk are both riding strong trends in the memory market, although they are involved in different aspects of it. What's even ...
news.AI_Summary:
• While Nvidia (NVDA) is the AI infrastructure leader, Micron Technology (MU) has gained 300% and Sandisk (SNDK) nearly 1,400% over the past year.
• Micron and Sandisk are benefiting from strong trends in the memory market, particularly High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI chips.
• Micron trades at a forward P/E of 3.7 times fiscal 2027 estimates, while Sandisk trades at an 8 times multiple for fiscal 2027.
• The memory market is cyclical, but the current supercycle is backed by structural AI growth, especially for HBM.
• DRAM contracts are shifting to longer terms (3-5 years) due to HBM demand, benefiting memory makers.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, NVDA, Micron, MU, Sandisk, SNDK, AI, memory market, HBM, DRAM, valuation

news.Title: Why Plug Power Stock Jumped 26% in March
news.Content: Plug Power (NASDAQ: PLUG) was one of the biggest surprises of March. The stock that fell nearly 34% in the four months through February jumped 26.3% last month, according to data provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence . Plug Power is still a loss-making company, but two critical ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Plug Power (PLUG) stock jumped 26.3% in March, recovering from a nearly 34% decline through February.
• The company achieved its first-ever positive gross profit of $5.5 million (2.4% gross margin) in Q4, a significant improvement from negative margins previously.
• Revenue surged 17.6% year over year, beating analyst estimates, and cash burn declined by 26.5%.
• Analysts raised their outlook on Plug Power stock following the earnings report and leadership change.
• The company expects operating profitability by the end of 2027 and full profitability by the end of 2028, but dilution risks and loan program issues remain.

news.Title: Stocks Finish Sharply Higher on US-Iran Ceasefire
news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) on Wednesday closed up +2.51%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) closed up +2.85%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) closed up +2.90%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) rose +2.52%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) rose +2.90%. Global stock indexes settled sharply higher on Wednesday, with the S&P 500, the Dow Jones Industrial Average, and the Nasdaq 100 posting 4-week highs. The easing of geopolitical tensions bolstered market sentiment and fueled a risk-on rally in equity markets after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, and Iran pledged to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Global stock indexes rallied sharply on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 up +2.51%, Dow Jones Industrial Average up +2.85%, and Nasdaq 100 up +2.90%.
• The rally was fueled by easing geopolitical tensions after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran pledged to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
• Crude oil prices sank by over 15% to a 1.5-week low, easing inflation concerns and boosting bond markets.
• It remains uncertain if the ceasefire will lead to a permanent end to the Iran war, with concerns about Iran's nuclear program and ballistic missile arsenal.
• The market sentiment is cautiously optimistic, awaiting further negotiations and normalization of energy flows.
news.Keywords: Stock market, rally, Iran ceasefire, geopolitical tensions, oil prices, inflation, bond yields, market sentiment

news.Title: Entegris, Inc. (ENTG): Billionaire Stan Druckenmiller Recognizes Upside Potential of This Stock
news.Content: Entegris, Inc. (NASDAQ:ENTG) is one of Billionaire Stan Druckenmiller’s 10 Small and Mid-Cap Stock Picks with Huge Upside Potential . Duquesne Capital has disclosed a stake in Entegris, Inc. (NASDAQ:ENTG) stock in three filings over the past four years. The first of these ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Entegris (ENTG) is a key provider of materials integrity for next-generation chips, particularly for 2nm and 3nm nodes for AI processors.
• Hedge funds are increasing their stakes in ENTG due to its dominance in liquid filtration and high-purity chemicals required for chip manufacturing.
• The company generated $404 million in free cash flow in 2025 and is committed to reducing net leverage.
• Stan Druckenmiller's Duquesne Capital has disclosed a stake in Entegris, indicating recognition of its upside potential.
• The company's aggressive debt reduction plan and strong free cash flow are attractive to institutional investors.
news.Keywords: Entegris, ENTG, semiconductors, AI processors, materials integrity, hedge funds, free cash flow, debt reduction, Stan Druckenmiller

news.Title: 3 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks That Actually Benefit From Google's TurboQuant Breakthrough
news.Content: It's been a tough past couple of weeks for Micron Technology and Sandisk shareholders. Both computer memory chipmakers have been outright upended. Blame Google parent Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) , mostly. It introduced a set of algorithms it collectively calls TurboQuant back on March 24, that purport to allow artificial intelligence (AI) computing platforms to handle the same amount of work with just one-sixth the amount of (currently very scarce) physical memory usually needed. The news immediately undermined much of the memory pricing power that's been propping up both of these stocks. This unveiling, of course, has also worked ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Google's TurboQuant algorithms could allow AI computing platforms to handle the same workload with one-sixth the memory, potentially impacting memory chipmakers like Micron and Sandisk.
• Qualcomm (QCOM) could benefit as TurboQuant may make AI more affordable, potentially boosting interest in its Snapdragon processors for mobile devices and edge computing.
• Broadcom (AVGO), a data center networking specialist, is unlikely to be negatively impacted by TurboQuant; increased efficiency could even enhance demand for AI hardware.
• Bank of America and Morgan Stanley analysts suggest TurboQuant could improve ROI for hyperscale capex, potentially increasing demand for AI hardware.
• The development may make AI more accessible to enterprises, potentially benefiting companies that provide AI infrastructure.
news.Keywords: Google, Alphabet, TurboQuant, AI, memory chips, Qualcomm, QCOM, Broadcom, AVGO, hyperscale, efficiency

news.Title: The Coming 2026 Growth Surge (& How to Ride it)
news.Content: Investors Should Understand the Market Environment When it comes to stock market investing, there are countless ways to “skin the cat” and achieve trading profitability. Investors like Whale Rock Capital Management’s Alex Sacerdote focus on finding and investing in growth and technology stocks. Meanwhile, investors like Warren Buffett seek value stocks, which typically sport low valuations, steady businesses, and are eager to return cash to shareholders through dividends or stock buybacks. Although elite investors like Sacerdote and Buffett outperform their ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Artificial intelligence (AI) is seen as a generational innovation, with companies like OpenAI and Anthropic showing significant revenue growth.
• Many growth stocks are now profitable with reasonable valuations, unlike during the dot-com bubble.
• NVIDIA (NVDA) has its lowest forward P/E ratio in over 5 years, despite its multi-year advance.
• Sandisk (SNDK) is cited as an example of robust price and volume action in 2026.
• Micron (MU) is highlighted for its liquidity, high growth, and visibility, being sold out of its entire High Bandwidth Memory production for 2026.
news.Keywords: AI, growth stocks, valuation, Nvidia, NVDA, Sandisk, SNDK, Micron, MU, momentum, liquidity

news.Title: Zuckerberg's AI Bet Yields First Proprietary Model
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Meta Platforms ( NASDAQ:META ) unveiled Muse Spark, its first AI model since CEO Mark ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms (META) unveiled Muse Spark, its first AI model from its Superintelligence Lab, designed for its family of apps.
• Muse Spark is a smaller, closed model initially available via Meta AI app and website, with plans for open-sourcing future versions.
• The model reportedly beats leading models from Alphabet (GOOGL), OpenAI, and Anthropic in select benchmarks for reasoning and multimodal capabilities.
• Meta is investing between $115 billion and $135 billion in AI in 2026, up from $72.22 billion in 2025.
• Meta's stock rose 7.55% intraday on the news of the Muse Spark AI model debut.
news.Keywords: Meta Platforms, META, AI model, Muse Spark, Superintelligence Lab, LLM, benchmarks, investment, stock performance

news.Title: Tesla Faces $43.9 Billion Free Cash Flow Swing As 2026 Outlook Turns Negative
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Tesla ( NASDAQ:TSLA ) is heading into what could be a difficult earnings setup with a massive reset in forward expectations that is hard to ignore. Consensus projections ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's (TSLA) forward expectations for 2026 free cash flow have swung dramatically, from a peak of $38.8 billion to a projected negative $5.1 billion.
• This shift takes Tesla from a self-funding business to potentially burning cash, with the magnitude matching its cash balance and exceeding historical free cash flow.
• Despite deteriorating financial projections, Tesla's valuation multiple has expanded to around 178 times expected earnings.
• Investor narratives focus on robotaxis, AI, and humanoid robots, even with missed targets and uncertainties.
• Q1 deliveries missed forecasts at 358,023 vehicles, with a record 50,000+ unsold vehicles, implying a working capital headwind.
news.Keywords: Tesla, TSLA, free cash flow, valuation, AI, robotaxis, deliveries, inventory, financial projections

news.Title: Why BigBear.ai Stock Sank 11.1% Last Month and Has Kept Falling in April
news.Content: BigBear.ai (NYSE: BBAI) stock got hit with another round of double-digit monthly sell-off in March. The company's share price fell 11.2% in the month, according to data from S&P Global Market Intelligence . Over the same period, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite declined 5.1% and 4.8%, respectively. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• BigBear.ai (BBAI) stock fell 11.2% in March and continued to decline in April, down roughly 36% year to date.
• The stock's pullback is attributed to broader market volatility related to the Iran war and an uninspiring quarterly report.
• Bearish analyst coverage also pressured the stock, with price target downgrades from H.C. Wainwright and Cantor Fitzgerald.
• BigBear.ai's specialization in defense technologies did not shield it from sell-offs amid broader market risk aversion.
• The company reported Q4 sales of $27.3 million, down roughly 38% year over year, missing analyst estimates.
news.Keywords: BigBear.ai, BBAI, defense stocks, AI, market volatility, quarterly report, analyst ratings, sales decline

news.Title: Why Palantir Technologies Stock Tanked on Wednesday
news.Content: In the face of a broader market recovery today, Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) was under pressure, with the stock falling as much as 7.3%. As of 3:51 p.m. ET, the stock was still down 6.6%. The catalyst that sent ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock fell as much as 7.3% amid a broader market recovery, influenced by a bleak outlook from investor Michael Burry.
• Burry, known for predicting the 2008 crash, has taken significant short positions in Palantir and Nvidia, betting on their prices to fall.
• Burry claims AI startup Anthropic is 'eating Palantir's lunch,' citing Anthropic's ARR growth from $9 billion to $30 billion.
• Palantir's Q4 revenue climbed 70% year over year, with its U.S. commercial segment growing 137%, driving adjusted EPS up 79%.
• Despite Burry's criticism, the author believes Palantir's results and accelerating revenue growth make it a buy.
news.Keywords: Palantir, PLTR, Nvidia, NVDA, Michael Burry, short position, AI, revenue growth, Anthropic

news.Title: Why Is Intel Stock Jumping Today
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Intel ( NASDAQ:INTC ) advanced about 8% on Wednesday, lifted by ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel (INTC) advanced about 8% on Wednesday, boosted by a positive analyst assessment and broad strength in technology shares.
• Analyst KC Rajkumar suggested Intel has resolved client wafer supply problems that impacted its Q1 outlook, with unfulfilled demand potentially carrying into future quarters.
• Activity at Intel's 7nm and 14nm nodes appears robust, running at full utilization, with supply shortfall potentially reaching 50% of current demand.
• Lynx Equity raised its price target on Intel to $70 from $65, citing improved near-term execution and longer-term potential.
• The analyst questioned Intel's buyback of its Ireland fab stake, speculating about an external customer's involvement.
news.Keywords: Intel, INTC, wafer supply, analyst assessment, price target, utilization, supply shortfall, AI infrastructure

news.Title: 4檔低價人氣股 法人青睞
news.Content: 台股千金族群熱鬧非凡，部分低價股依然能吸引研究機構目光，面板雙虎8日再度榮膺個股成交量冠、亞軍，法人正面看待轉型契機；記憶體族群中的華邦電（2344）、力積電（6770）股 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 低價股仍受法人青睞，面板雙虎群創、友達成交量居冠、亞軍，法人正面看待轉型契機。
• 記憶體族群華邦電、力積電獲投顧及摩根士丹利青睞，看好股價漲升空間。
• 群創受惠傳統運動賽事需求，品牌客戶提前拉貨，第二季報價溫和上漲，有助去化產能並改善毛利。
• 友達推動雙軸轉型，增加高附加價值產品及擴展垂直場域事業，兆豐投顧給予「逢低買進」投資評等。
• 力積電因記憶體客戶需求續強，晶圓出貨量與單位售價走揚，摩根士丹利看好其前景。
news.Keywords: 低價股,面板雙虎,記憶體,華邦電,力積電,群創,友達,轉型,AI

news.Title: 華邦電、群聯 首季營收同步報喜
news.Content:
AI應用由訓練端快速走向推論端，帶動記憶體與儲存需求同步升溫，華邦電（2344）與群聯（8299）8日同步公布3月與第一季營收成績，雙雙繳出改寫歷史新高的亮眼成績單。

華邦電3 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI應用帶動記憶體與儲存需求升溫，華邦電與群聯3月及第一季營收均創歷史新高。
• 華邦電3月營收年增91.49%，第一季營收年增91.34%，連續四個月改寫單月新高。
• 群聯3月營收年增221%，第一季營收年增196%，創單月及同期新高。
• 法人指出，AI發展重新定義記憶體價值，產業轉向重視效能、算力與大容量配置。
• 華邦電2026年資本支出將大增至421億元，用於擴產，Flash與客製化記憶體解決方案為擴產重點。
news.Keywords: AI,記憶體,儲存,華邦電,群聯,營收創新高,資本支出,擴產

news.Title: 美伊停火二周》台股漲點 史上第二
news.Content: 美國與伊朗停火兩周，帶動台股在內的亞股8日強漲。台股方面，集三大法人千億資金全力湧進，尤其外資報復性回補、史上第二大買超1,177億元搶進，鎖定台積電以 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美伊停火兩周，帶動台股在內的亞股8日強漲，台股加權指數終場上漲1,531點至34,761點，創史上第二大漲點。
• 外資報復性回補、史上第二大買超1,177億元搶進，鎖定台積電以史上第七大買超357億元敲單。
• 三大法人8日同步站在買方，合計買超台股1,583億元，自營商買超358.68億元創歷史最多。
• 台股8日長紅K棒突破三角收斂區，看好4月有望挑戰歷史新高35,579點。
• 市場資金將聚焦於強勢股，如矽光子、光通訊、檢測等，台積電法說會概念股有望吸引目光。
news.Keywords: 美伊停火,台股,大漲,外資,買超,台積電,法說會,AI概念股

• 美伊停火兩周，台股迎來報復性反彈，多檔主動台股ETF率先創高。
• 台灣企業掌握全球約90%AI伺服器市占及70%半導體代工市占，具備替代性低的競爭條件。
• 半導體身為AI科技之母，台廠半導體產業鏈扮演AI晶片領頭羊與AI基建軍火庫。
• 預計台積電法說將釋出產業景氣暢旺風向球，半導體行業價格上行趨勢明確。
• AI資本支出與高效能運算需求持續擴張，台股中長期多頭架構未變，科技型ETF仍是市場配置重心。

news.Content: 美伊達成停火共識，8日台股多頭火力猛攻，千金股百花齊放，台股40千金達陣，寫歷史新紀錄，早盤宜鼎、AES-KY、金像電 ...

news.Title: 台股被動ETF旺 前十強犀利
news.Content: 美伊戰爭現轉機，美國總統川普7日宣布，延後兩周對伊朗採取大規模軍事行動，伊朗隨後表示，接受為期兩周的停火協議，國際油價瞬間暴跌17％，台股 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美伊戰爭現轉機，台股加權指數8日大漲1,531點，重返34,000點關卡之上。
• 台股被動ETF績效勁揚，8日績效前十強皆大漲6%以上，較加權指數超額報酬達3成以上。
• 市場資金正以最直接的方式回應AI浪潮的下一場演進，全球資金透過「產品化」布局記憶體產業。
• 台灣記憶體供應鏈在全球AI基礎設施中的關鍵地位，已納入美國記憶體主題ETF。
• AI產業需求展望仍是支撐市場長期趨勢的核心動能，儘管短期有地緣政治干擾，但中長期具結構性投資機會。
news.Keywords: 台股ETF,AI,記憶體,美伊停火,油價,通膨,地緣政治,科技股

news.Title: 00400A掛牌 搶搭台股順風車
news.Content: 美伊宣布停火兩周令避險情緒衰退，油價自高檔回落，亞股普遍飆漲，台股8日大漲1,531點，收34,761點。國泰投信指出，市場現處於短期情緒修復期，後續通膨壓力與風險資產 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美伊宣布停火兩周，避險情緒衰退，油價回落，亞股普遍飆漲，台股8日大漲1,531點，收34,761點。
• 面對變幻莫測的金融情勢，被動布局已難滿足追求超額報酬目標，主動式台股ETF熱度飆升。
• 國泰動能高息ETF（00400A）結合「收益防守」與「動能成長」，篩選優質殖利率標的並強調發展潛力。
• 主動式ETF結合專業團隊選股策略與ETF交易便利性，能在市場波動中動態布局。
• 00400A主打每月收益分配機制，讓投資人分享資本增值並緩解波動焦慮。
news.Keywords: ETF,主動式ETF,被動式ETF,台股,AI,高息股,月配息,資產配置

news.Title: 12檔主動台股ETF 績效勝大盤
news.Content: 美伊為期兩周的停火、原油價格跌破每桶100美元利多下，台股應聲大漲1,531.56點，市場投資新寵主動式台股 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美伊停火利多下，台股應聲大漲1,531.56點，主動式台股ETF表現更出色，單日與今年來平均漲幅雙雙打敗大盤。
• AI相關科技產業及其供應鏈，包括電源管理、高效能運算基礎設施及半導體設備廠務工程，具較高成長潛力。
• 基本面穩健的產業與族群仍具備顯著的布局價值，預期載板、設備、半導體、光通訊、記憶體等族群將持續受惠。
• 台灣正處於AI應用普及化、半導體供應鏈重組等重大結構性變革中，需配置具護城河、技術領先、獲利動能上修、高現金流企業。
• 雲端服務公司持續加碼AI基礎設施，台灣電子業高階晶片、伺服器、記憶體與散熱等供應鏈環節具優勢，預期持續受惠雲端資本支出循環。

news.Title: 外資：台積季季旺到2027年底
news.Content: 台積電法說前熱度持續加溫，花旗環球、摩根士丹利、高盛及摩根大通證券等攜手凝聚市場焦點，並展現對台積電營運動能高度信心，最吸睛之處在於，據外資估算，台積電單季營收從去 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 外資看好台積電營運動能，預估單季營收將持續成長到2027年底，呈現「季季旺」。
• 儘管智慧手機半導體訂單出現調整，但AI與網通等高效能運算客戶需求強勁，可有效填補產能。
• 預期台積電第二季營收將季增5%～10%，毛利率維持約64%～65%。
• 高盛將台積電目標價上調至2,750元，主要受AI需求主導，營收年增率預估上調至35%（2026年）與30%（2027年）。
• 台積電3奈米與5奈米製程產能利用率滿載，輔以生產效率持續改善，營運動能強勁。
news.Keywords: 台積電,法說會,外資,目標價,AI,營收,毛利率,先進製程,資本支出

news.Title: 美伊停火二周》台股漲點 史上第二
news.Content: 美國與伊朗停火兩周，帶動台股在內的亞股8日強漲。台股方面，集三大法人千億資金全力湧進，尤其外資報復性回補、史上第二大買超1,177億元搶進，鎖定台積電以 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美伊停火兩周，帶動台股在內的亞股8日強漲，台股加權指數終場上漲1,531點至34,761點，創史上第二大漲點。
• 外資報復性回補、史上第二大買超1,177億元搶進，鎖定台積電以史上第七大買超357億元敲單。
• 三大法人8日同步站在買方，合計買超台股1,583億元，自營商買超358.68億元創歷史最多。
• 台股8日長紅K棒突破三角收斂區，看好4月有望挑戰歷史新高35,579點。
• 市場資金將聚焦於強勢股，如矽光子、光通訊、檢測等，台積電法說會概念股有望吸引目光。
news.Keywords: 美伊停火,台股,大漲,外資,買超,台積電,法說會,AI概念股

news.Content: 半導體設備廠弘塑（3131）業績跟著台積電（2330）起舞，伴隨著台積電 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 半導體設備廠弘塑業績跟隨台積電擴產，訂單滿手，今年營運續看漲。
• 法人預估弘塑今年設備出貨量將達200至250台，營收將雙位數成長。
• 下半年新廠投產，外包比例下降，有助獲利能力提升。
• 弘塑2月營收維持強勁成長動能，單月營收年增51.8%，累計前二月營收年增47%。

news.Title: Time to Buy the Dip on Texas Pacific Land Stock?
news.Content: Participate in financial markets long enough, and an investor is apt to hear the saying, "Stocks don't move up in a straight line." Even in the strongest-trending bull markets, hot stocks take breathers. Due to the war in Iran, it's felt as though some energy stocks moved higher in a straight-line fashion. Texas Pacific Land (NYSE: TPL) exuded such ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Texas Pacific Land (TPL) shares have plunged 17.1% in the month ended April 2, extending its retreat from its 52-week high by 18.8%.
• Despite the pullback, shares are up over 54% year to date, but have slipped below their 50-day moving average.
• TPL operates a 'toll road' business model, collecting revenue from oil, water, and pipelines on its West Texas real estate, not directly tied to crude prices.
• The company is pivoting to bolster water desalination capabilities and has AI inroads through energy data hubs.
• Horizon Kinetics, a major investor, believes TPL has more upside ahead, factoring in data center opportunities and renewable energy exposure.
news.Keywords: Texas Pacific Land, TPL, energy, water, pipelines, AI, data centers, toll road model, business pivot

news.Title: Stop Worrying About CapEx and Buy the Dip in Amazon Stock Here
news.Content: Again, the tech giant Amazon (AMZN) has become the center of the larger discussion about whether Big Tech companies might be overreaching in their ambitions to control the artificial intelligence market. The company's plan to invest in capital expenditure worth up to $200 billion by 2026 certainly makes investors uneasy, as they have trouble reconciling such high expenses with free cash flow problems. Nevertheless, this seems to be ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon (AMZN) plans to invest up to $200 billion in capital expenditure by 2026 to build AI infrastructure and secure compute capacity.
• Despite concerns about high expenses and free cash flow, Amazon's capex is seen as positioning for the rapidly growing AI market.
• AWS revenue rose 24% year-over-year to $35.6 billion in Q4 2025, showing strong cloud growth.
• Amazon's net sales grew 14% YoY to $213.4 billion in Q4 2025, with operating income rising 18.3%.
• The company's stock is trading at a P/E ratio of 29.68, with analysts seeing potential upside and strong financials.
news.Keywords: Amazon, AMZN, AI, capital expenditures, AWS, cloud computing, revenue growth, profitability, valuation

news.Title: Apple Turns To Samsung For Foldable Screens
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Apple ( NASDAQ:AAPL ) is reportedly turning to Samsung Display as its sole ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple (AAPL) is reportedly turning to Samsung Display as its sole supplier for foldable iPhone screens due to industry readiness.
• Apple has agreed to a 3-year exclusive deal for foldable OLED panels, as other suppliers like BOE and LG Display are not yet meeting Apple's standards.
• Shipments are expected to reach around 3 million units by the end of 2026, lower than earlier projections.
• The foldable iPhone is expected to be priced premium, starting near $2,325, targeting early adopters.
• This exclusivity could run until 2029 or 2030, depending on competitor catch-up.
news.Keywords: Apple, AAPL, foldable iPhone, Samsung Display, supplier, OLED panels, production, pricing, exclusivity

news.Title: 3 Top Agentic AI Stocks Investors Should Be Loading Up on Now
news.Content: Agentic artificial intelligence (AI) is the next major step in the AI revolution. It allows users to develop AI agents that can perform tasks on their behalf with limited human direction. While the most widely used applications of this technology now involve coding, it could easily expand to other areas over the next few years. Getting in front of trends like this can be a profitable pursuit for investors, so now would be ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) leads in parallel processing chips (GPUs) and supports AI workloads with its infrastructure.
• Broadcom (AVGO) targets the market for custom AI chips (ASICs) designed for specific workloads like AI inferencing, offering cost-performance advantages over GPUs.
• Alphabet (GOOGL) launched Gemma 4, a free AI agent model, aiming to lower the barrier to entry for AI agent creation and capture long-term customers for paid services.
• As AI agents become mainstream, they will require more computing hardware from both Nvidia and Broadcom.
• Alphabet's strategy of offering free AI tools could drive adoption and future revenue.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, NVDA, Broadcom, AVGO, Alphabet, GOOGL, AI agents, GPUs, ASICs, TPUs, cloud computing

news.Title: The AI "Capex Trap" Is Wall Street's Biggest Fear Right Now. Here's Why I Think It's Overblown.
news.Content: The most significant challenge with stocks in the artificial intelligence (AI) space involves capital expenditures (capex). Some of the top tech companies plan to spend over $100 billion each on capex in 2026 after investing massive amounts into it last year. Though companies such as Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) or Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG) hold massive liquidity positions, even the largest tech giants are not immune to investors' concerns about the size of their outlays. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The AI market is projected to grow significantly, with Grand View Research forecasting a CAGR of 31% through 2033, reaching nearly $3.5 trillion.
• Amazon (AMZN) plans $200 billion in capex for 2026, following $132 billion in 2025, yet its financial position has improved with revenue up 12% and AWS revenue up 20%.
• Alphabet (GOOGL) plans $175-$185 billion in capex for 2026, up from $91 billion in 2025, with revenue up 18% and Google Cloud revenue up 48%.
• Despite massive capex, both Amazon and Alphabet generated substantial free cash flow and increased book value, suggesting returns on investment.
• The article argues that rising capex spending is not a 'trap' but a buying opportunity if stock prices fall amid these financial improvements.
news.Keywords: AI, capex, Amazon, AMZN, Alphabet, GOOGL, cloud computing, revenue growth, free cash flow, investment opportunity

news.Title: Cathie Wood buys trending tech stocks ahead of U.S.-Iran ceasefire
news.Content: Veteran investor Cathie Wood runs one of the most active funds that regularly trades in crypto-linked equities. A pioneer in the world of crypto investing, she once revealed that her fund was the first public asset manager to gain Bitcoin (BTC) exposure in 2015 when the cryptocurrency wasn't worth even $500. Related: Cathie Wood has brutal response ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Cathie Wood's ARK Invest purchased shares of Tesla (TSLA) and Robinhood Markets (HOOD) on April 7.
• Tesla holds 11,509 Bitcoin worth $827.5 million, having previously accepted Bitcoin payments but abandoned the policy due to environmental concerns.
• Robinhood offers cryptocurrencies and tokenized stocks, and will assist the U.S. Treasury Department with the Trump Accounts program.
• Tesla's stock was up 0.06% at $346.86, and Robinhood's stock was up 5% at $73.13 at press time.
• Wood's purchases occurred on the same day President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran.
news.Keywords: Cathie Wood, ARK Invest, Tesla, TSLA, Robinhood, HOOD, Bitcoin, cryptocurrency, Iran ceasefire

news.Title: 重電股／清明後台股上攻？　東元新報告
news.Content:
財經中心／廖珪如報導

法人最新報告指出，機電大廠東元（1504）受惠電氣化與資料中心需求成長，Busway與電力設備業務動能延續，但模組化資料中心（MDC）整體解決方案仍處 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 東元受惠電氣化與資料中心需求成長，Busway與電力設備業務動能延續。
• 模組化資料中心（MDC）整體解決方案仍處初期推展階段，短期貢獻有限，目標價下修至65元。
• 預估2026年營收達650.98億元，年增10.2%，主要受惠光纖基礎建設帶動機電需求。
• 2026年毛利率預估25.1%，年增1.3個百分點，但略低於預期，主因工程與系統業務占比提升。
• 展望2027年，隨模組化業務逐步放量，營收預估達773.69億元，年增18.8%，EPS挑戰3.26元。
news.Keywords: 東元,電氣化,資料中心,MDC,營收,毛利率,EPS,目標價下修

news.Title: Everyone's Buying NVIDIA - Here Are 2 Smarter AI Stocks for Q2 2026
news.Content: For years, NVIDIA Corporation NVDA has been a Wall Street darling, with its shares scaling upward due to the relentless demand for its cutting-edge chips and graphics processing units (GPUs). However, despite a strong interest in its products, this year has been subdued for the Jensen Huang-led company. Geopolitical tensions and anxiety over a potential cut in spending on artificial intelligence ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) and Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) are poised to gain from hyperscalers' significant capital spending on AI.
• Micron's high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips are in high demand due to their ability to handle heavy workloads and improve power efficiency.
• Micron projects fiscal Q3 2026 revenues of $33.5 billion with an expected high gross margin of 81%, reflecting strong financial momentum.
• Broadcom's AI revenues surged 106% year-over-year to $8.4 billion in fiscal Q1 2026, with AI semiconductor revenue expected to reach $10.7 billion in fiscal Q2 2026.
• Both Micron and Broadcom have Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) ratings, with analysts forecasting significant price target increases.
news.Keywords: Micron, MU, Broadcom, AVGO, AI, hyperscalers, HBM, memory demand, revenue growth, gross margin, price target

news.Title: AI產業下一個瓶頸：為何美國製造的頂級晶片仍必須往返台灣
news.Content:
隨著 AI 產業對運算效能的需求飆升，先進封裝這個長期遭忽視的半導體環節，正迅速成為全球科技供應鏈的下一個致命瓶頸，

《CNBC》報導，儘管當前輿論焦點多集中在晶圓製造，但實際上所有驅動 AI 的晶片，最終都必須透過精密的封裝 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI產業對先進封裝需求飆升，成為全球科技供應鏈的下一個瓶頸。
• 台積電CoWoS先進封裝需求大幅增長，複合年成長率高達80%，輝達已鎖定大部分產能。
• 日月光預估其2026年先進封裝業務銷售額將翻倍，顯示市場熱度高。
• 美國試圖將供應鏈本土化，台積電亞利桑那州封裝廠、英特爾投資成為焦點。
• 台積電雖在亞利桑那州設晶圓廠，但100%晶片仍需運回台灣封裝，增加物流時間與地緣政治風險。
news.Keywords: AI,先進封裝,台積電,CoWoS,日月光,輝達,供應鏈,美國製造,地緣政治風險

news.Title: Forget SpaceX: Is RocketLab Stock the Better Buy?
news.Content: Elon Musk's space exploration brainchild, SpaceX , is set to hit the market through an initial public offering (IPO) expected in June. With Bloomberg reporting a target valuation of over $2 trillion, it would become one of the world's largest public companies. But is it a good investment? Let's compare SpaceX to a smaller alternative, RocketLab (NASDAQ: RKLB) , to see which stock might make the better long-term bet. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX is targeting a valuation of over $2 trillion for its IPO, potentially making it one of the world's largest public companies.
• Rocket Lab (RKLB) is presented as a smaller alternative with potentially more room for growth, having a market cap of $37 billion.
• SpaceX's revenue grew 18% in 2025, a deceleration from 64% growth in 2024, while Rocket Lab could boost operations with its new Neutron rocket.
• SpaceX faces political exposure due to Elon Musk's leadership, potentially impacting regulatory permissions and government contracts.
• Rocket Lab's Neutron program has faced delays, posing a risk to its commercial operation timeline and profitability expectations.
news.Keywords: SpaceX, IPO, Rocket Lab, RKLB, valuation, growth, political exposure, Neutron rocket, delays

news.Title: Apple Shares Rise on Wedbush Bullish Call
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Apple ( NASDAQ:AAPL ) climbed about 2% on Wednesday, lifted by a U.S.-Iran ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple (AAPL) shares rose about 2% on Wednesday, boosted by a U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement and bullish commentary from Wedbush on Mac demand.
• Wedbush analyst Matt Bryson noted extended lead times for Mac mini (2-3 months) and Mac Studio (2-3 weeks) reflect strong demand.
• This strength is notable given overall PC shipment slumps due to higher memory costs.
• Apple may be gaining share in the handset market as it does not appear to be reducing builds like peers.
• Some analysts attribute Mac mini demand to OpenClaw, an open-source project for running agentic AI tasks locally.
news.Keywords: Apple, AAPL, Mac, demand, lead times, Wedbush, AI, OpenClaw, market share

news.Title: Micron Deepens AI Push With SiMa.ai Deal
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Micron Technology ( NASDAQ:MU ) is leaning further into ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) is deepening its AI push by investing in SiMa.ai to scale physical AI systems.
• The partnership focuses on efficiently combining compute and memory for edge use cases, leveraging Micron's LPDDR5X memory.
• SiMa.ai's platform integrates Micron memory for better bandwidth and lower power consumption in robotics and autonomous systems.
• The broader trend is toward physical AI, embedding intelligence into machines operating in real environments, where performance-per-watt is critical.
• Micron's expanded role in AI infrastructure highlights the importance of memory integration for AI performance.
news.Keywords: Micron, MU, AI, SiMa.ai, physical AI, edge computing, memory integration, performance-per-watt, robotics

news.Title: Veteran Analyst Says Microsoft, Salesforce to Lead Tech Rally After Ceasefire
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . The Magnificent Seven and broader technology sector are likely to benefit from a risk-on ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Wedbush believes Microsoft (MSFT), Salesforce, and ServiceNow are well-positioned to benefit from a risk-on environment following the Iran ceasefire.
• The firm sees the sell-off in software stocks as overblown, with companies integrating AI rather than being replaced by it.
• Concerns about AI incumbents shifting to enterprise software infrastructure are considered a stretch.
• Revenue generation will focus on partnering around AI workflows, according to CIOs surveyed by Wedbush.
• Microsoft's Azure AI services and Copilot business are benefiting from AI integration, with a large remaining performance obligation (RPO) of $625 billion.
news.Keywords: Microsoft, MSFT, Salesforce, ServiceNow, AI, software stocks, ceasefire, cloud computing, RPO, workflow

news.Title: Market indexes soared on the Iran truce, but risks remain
news.Content: The top market indexes all rose today, thanks to a two-week truce in the war with Iran. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) , the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) , and the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) were up by 2.3%-2.9% as of this writing ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Major market indexes rose significantly due to a two-week truce in the Iran war, with the S&P 500, Nasdaq Composite, and Dow Jones Industrial Average all gaining.
• Alphabet (GOOG) and Meta Platforms (META) were key drivers of the tech sector's gains, with META rallying 8% and GOOG up 4%.
• Meta launched its new large language model, Spark, which should help scale its AI tools faster than the previous Llama system.
• Despite the day's gains, Meta is down 6% year-to-date, and Alphabet is up only 2%.
• The ceasefire is viewed positively, but risks remain due to the fragile nature of the truce and ongoing restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz.
news.Keywords: Market indexes, Iran truce, Alphabet, GOOGL, Meta Platforms, META, AI, LLM, Spark, year-to-date performance

news.Title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview Just Sent Shockwaves Through the Cybersecurity Industry. It's Now Joining Forces with Nvidia, Amazon, Apple, Google, and Microsoft
news.Content: Experts have long said that artificial intelligence (AI) would change the face of cybersecurity. While many industry leaders have already deployed AI tools to prevent hacks, data breaches, and cyberattacks, the potential for sophisticated intrusions just took a giant leap forward. AI start-up Anthropic unveiled the Claude Mythos Preview, its latest frontier AI model. This ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI startup Anthropic unveiled Claude Mythos Preview, identifying thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across critical infrastructure.
• This revelation sparked Project Glasswing, a collaboration including Nvidia (NVDA), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Alphabet (GOOGL), Broadcom (AVGO), Microsoft (MSFT), and Cisco (CSCO) to address these vulnerabilities.
• The initiative aims to secure critical infrastructure against AI-powered attacks by plugging identified gaps.
• Mythos Preview found high-severity vulnerabilities in major operating systems and web browsers.
• This broad coalition highlights the seriousness of AI's impact on cybersecurity and the ongoing age of AI development.
news.Keywords: Anthropic, Claude Mythos, AI, cybersecurity, zero-day vulnerabilities, Project Glasswing, collaboration, Nvidia, Amazon, Apple, Alphabet, Broadcom, Microsoft, Cisco

news.Title: Applied Materials Just Launched a New Chipmaking System. Should You Buy AMAT Stock Here?
news.Content: Applied Materials (AMAT) stock is extending gains on Wednesday morning after the firm announced a breakthrough in transistor manufacturing. On April 8, the semiconductor equipment manufacturer launched two new deposition systems — the Precision Selective Nitride PECVD and Trillium ALD ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Applied Materials (AMAT) launched two new deposition systems, Precision Selective Nitride PECVD and Trillium ALD, for 2nm and 'angstrom-era' logic chips.
• These systems position AMAT as a key enabler for next-generation semiconductors like Gate-All-Around (GAA) transistors.
• AMAT's tools solve critical atomic-scale challenges in depositing metals and dielectrics with angstrom-level precision.
• The company secured a foothold in the 2nm process node, locking in multi-year sales streams from foundries like TSMC and Samsung.
• AMAT's stock is up nearly 45% year-to-date, trades at a forward P/E of 31x, and has a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).
news.Keywords: Applied Materials, AMAT, semiconductors, chipmaking, deposition systems, 2nm process, GAA transistors, AI infrastructure, valuation

news.Title: Analysts See Massive Upside in Microsoft: Time to Buy MSFT Stock?
news.Content: With a loss of over 23% as of April 7 closing prices, Microsoft is the worst-performing Magnificent 7 stock this year. The stock is also underperforming its average S&P 500 Index ($SPX) peer this year, something it also did in the previous two years. Notably, while Microsoft was seen as a defensive play given its relatively stable business compared to other Big Tech companies, it failed to live up to that perception this year and has underperformed ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft (MSFT) is the worst-performing Magnificent 7 stock year-to-date, down over 23%, and underperforming its S&P 500 peers.
• Goldman Sachs reiterated a 'Buy' rating with a $600 target price, implying 61% upside, while Benchmark initiated coverage with a 'Buy' and $450 target.
• Microsoft's stock is down nearly 33% from its 52-week highs, with concerns over ballooning capex for AI infrastructure and Azure's decelerating growth (39% in fiscal Q2 2026).
• Concentration risk exists with OpenAI accounting for 45% of Microsoft's remaining performance obligations (RPOs).
• Despite these concerns, analysts remain bullish due to Microsoft's strong underlying fundamentals and potential AI monetization.
news.Keywords: Microsoft, MSFT, Magnificent 7, stock performance, analyst ratings, capex, Azure, OpenAI, RPO, AI

news.Title: 2 Tech Stocks That Could Help Set You Up for Life
news.Content: Technology stocks were a boon for investors in recent years thanks to the arrival of artificial intelligence (AI). But Wall Street's appetite for the sector ebbed in 2026, as concerns arose that artificial intelligence would displace many software companies. This triggered a sell-off among software-as-a-service (SaaS) stocks, leading to share price declines for ...
news.AI_Summary:
• ServiceNow (NOW) shares fell over 30% in 2026 through April 1 due to market rotation away from software stocks amid AI fears.
• The company reported strong Q4 2025 performance, with net new annual contract values (ACVs) exceeding $1 million growing nearly 40% YoY.
• Q4 sales rose 21% year over year to $3.6 billion, with its Now Assist AI product's net new ACV doubling year over year.
• Workiva (WK) shares also plunged 30% this year due to AI fears, but reported Q4 2025 sales up 20% year over year to $238.9 million, achieving net income.
• Both stocks have attractive valuations with forward earnings multiples near their low points for the past year, presenting a buying opportunity.
news.Keywords: ServiceNow, NOW, Workiva, WK, AI, software stocks, valuation, earnings growth, contract value, buying opportunity

news.Title: Broadcom Just Landed Game-Changing AI Deals. Is AVGO a Buy Now?
news.Content: Broadcom (AVGO) has grabbed investor attention after securing major multi-year AI chip deals with Alphabet’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google and rising AI powerhouse Anthropic. The stock climbed 6.2% yesterday with the expectation that these deals will solidify Broadcom’s position as the key backbone of AI infrastructure for years. They signal long-term visibility into both revenue and relevance in the rapidly expanding AI chip market. AVGO stock is up just 1% year-to-date (YTD), compared to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom (AVGO) secured major multi-year AI chip deals with Google and Anthropic, boosting its position as a key backbone of AI infrastructure.
• These deals signal long-term revenue visibility and relevance in the AI chip market, with AVGO stock up 1% YTD.
• Broadcom signed a deal with Google to supply custom AI chips (TPUs) until 2031 and partnered with Anthropic for AI compute capacity access.
• AI semiconductor revenues surged 106% YoY to $8.4 billion in fiscal Q1 2026, with management expecting AI revenue to reach $10.7 billion in fiscal Q2 2026.
• Analysts maintain a 'Strong Buy' rating on AVGO, with price targets suggesting significant upside potential.
news.Keywords: Broadcom, AVGO, AI deals, Google, Anthropic, TPU, AI infrastructure, revenue growth, analyst ratings, upside potential

news.Title: Better Altcoin Buy: XRP vs. TAO

After all, XRP is down nearly 30% for the year, while Bittensor is up more ...
news.AI_Summary:
• XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) and Bittensor (CRYPTO: TAO) are identified as altcoins with high upside potential in the crypto market.
• XRP has a market opportunity in the global payment system, potentially replacing SWIFT and capturing significant transaction volume.
• Ripple has invested nearly $3 billion in blockchain and crypto transactions since 2023 to build an XRP-centric global payment system.

news.Title: Investing $10,000 In Each of These 3 Growth Stocks 5 Years Ago Would Have Created a Portfolio Worth More Than $260,000 Today
news.Content: The big appeal with investing in growth stocks is for the potential upside they possess. Dividend stocks may offer safety and dividend income, but if you're looking for big returns, you'll want to own growth stocks. It's not always obvious which growth stocks will flourish, and that's why it can be a good move to invest in several of them. Five years ago, ChatGPT wasn't the household name that it is today. Generative artificial intelligence (AI) wasn't on the horizon, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) have been significant growth stocks over the past five years, driven by AI.
• An investment of $10,000 in each stock five years ago would now be worth over $260,000.
• Nvidia's stock has risen nearly 1,200% in five years, with its GPUs critical for AI model development.
• Broadcom's revenue totaled nearly $64 billion in fiscal year 2025, nearly doubling from a few years prior, driven by custom AI chips.
• Palantir Technologies' AI platform has helped companies find efficiencies, with strong growth in its government and commercial segments.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, NVDA, Broadcom, AVGO, Palantir Technologies, PLTR, AI, growth stocks, returns, valuation

news.Title: ARM Stock Up 32% YTD: Should You Wait for a Better Entry Point?
news.Content: Arm Holdings ARM sits at the center of the global semiconductor ecosystem, with its stock rocketing 32% so far this year. The company’s chip architecture powers billions of devices worldwide, particularly smartphones, and its licensing model allows it to generate scalable royalty streams without the heavy manufacturing costs faced by traditional chipmakers. Image Source: Zacks Investment Research However, while ARM’s ecosystem advantages remain formidable, valuation concerns and cyclical industry dynamics seem to be complicating the near-term picture. So, let's see if investors should buy ARM stock now or wait for ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings (ARM) stock is up 32% year-to-date, driven by its chip architecture powering billions of devices, particularly smartphones.
• ARM's licensing model generates scalable royalty streams without heavy manufacturing costs.
• The company reported adjusted earnings of 43 cents per share in Q4 2025, beating estimates by 4.9%, and quarterly revenues reached $1.24 billion, up 26.4% YoY.
• ARM's ecosystem creates a powerful competitive advantage through a two-sided network effect connecting developers and manufacturers.
• Morgan Stanley downgraded ARM stock due to valuation concerns, cyclical industry dynamics, and the company's shift into developing its own physical silicon for AI.
news.Keywords: Arm Holdings, ARM, semiconductors, chip architecture, smartphones, licensing, royalties, valuation, AI, downgrade

news.Title: Waymo ride quietly arrives in a new key U.S. city
news.Content: Imagine opening an app, booking a ride, and a vehicle with no driver shows up to take you to a long-awaited errand. That’s no longer a concept. It’s a reality in Nashville. Waymo, a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc., is a U.S.-based autonomous driving technology company headquartered in Mountain View, California. As of March 2026, Waymo operates commercial robotaxi services across 10 major U.S. metropolitan areas, with a fleet of about 3,000 vehicles. Waymo also delivers roughly 500,000 paid rides each week and has surpassed 200 million fully autonomous miles driven, proving its growing scale and leadership in self-driving technology. Waymo ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Waymo, an Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) subsidiary, has launched its fully autonomous ride-hailing service in Nashville, expanding its operations to 11 U.S. cities.
• Waymo operates commercial robotaxi services across 10 major U.S. cities with a fleet of about 3,000 vehicles, delivering roughly 500,000 paid rides weekly.
• The company plans to integrate its service with the Lyft platform later in 2026, with Lyft acting as a fleet management partner.
• Nashville's mix of tourism, nightlife, and growth makes it an ideal environment for autonomous transport.
• Waymo leads the U.S. robotaxi race but faces competition from Tesla, Amazon-owned Zoox, and startups like Waabi and Nuro.
news.Keywords: Waymo, Alphabet, GOOGL, autonomous driving, robotaxi, ride-hailing, Nashville, Lyft, competition

news.Title: Meta launches Muse Spark AI model as part of its AI turnaround
news.Content: Meta ( META ) on Wednesday launched what it said is its most powerful AI model yet, a key piece of its broader AI rebuilding effort. The model, called Muse Spark, is the first from the company’s Meta Superintelligence Lab (MSL), which CEO Mark Zuckerberg unveiled in June. Meta ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms (META) launched Muse Spark, its first AI model from its Superintelligence Lab, designed to catch up with rivals.
• The model is initially available on the Meta AI app and website, and will replace existing Llama models powering Meta's platforms.
• Muse Spark features multimodal reasoning, tool-use support, and multi-agent orchestration, aiming for capabilities competitive with Gemini and GPT Pro.
• Meta is investing heavily in AI, expecting to spend between $115 billion and $135 billion in 2026.
• Meta's stock rose more than 9% intraday on the news of the Muse Spark AI model debut.
news.Keywords: Meta Platforms, META, AI model, Muse Spark, Superintelligence Lab, LLM, investment, stock performance, competition

news.Title: 美伊停火重磅利多！台股創史上第二大漲點　盤中閃現「41千金」
news.Content:
財經中心／林浩博報導

受到美伊停戰的重磅利多激勵，台股今（8）日締造多項壯麗紀錄。加權指數在多頭總司令台積電帶領下，狂飆1531.56點 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美伊停戰重磅利多激勵，台股8日締造多項紀錄，加權指數狂飆1531.56點，創史上第二大收盤漲點。
• 盤中首度出現「40檔千金股」里程碑，PCB大廠金像電一度漲停，終場收998元。
• 股王信驊站穩萬點關卡，強勢衝上12,615元漲停；半導體測試介面廠穎崴亦紅燈鎖死。
• 14檔千元大股同步漲停，包括台光電、奇鋐、嘉澤、旺矽等，寫下史上最剽悍的高價股收盤表現。
• 隨國際油價重挫，塑化股成為盤面最弱勢族群，與千金股滿天飛形成強烈反差。
news.Keywords: 美伊停火,台股,漲點,千金股,信驊,穎崴,台光電,奇鋐,塑化股

news.Title: The Best Trillion-Dollar Stock to Buy Right Now, According to Wall Street
news.Content: While many investors are concerned about the growing concentration of large companies' stocks in the S&P 500 , most analysts seem to think the trillion-dollar behemoths could get even bigger relative to the rest of the market. They see tremendous upside in most megacap stocks based on their price targets. Increased spending on artificial intelligence (AI) has driven the valuations of many of the largest companies in the world, and that trend isn't slowing down anytime soon. Semiconductor stocks , including Nvidia , Broadcom , and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing , ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft (MSFT) is facing concerns over its $37.5 billion quarterly capex for AI infrastructure and decelerating Azure revenue growth (39%).
• Competitors Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) are showing accelerating revenue growth in their cloud segments (48% and 24% respectively).
• Investors are also concerned about generative AI disrupting Microsoft's enterprise software business.
• Despite these headwinds, analysts maintain a bullish outlook, seeing 61% upside potential to a $600 price target.
• Microsoft's stock is down over 30% from its highs, presenting a potential buying opportunity.
news.Keywords: Microsoft, MSFT, AI, capex, Azure, cloud growth, enterprise software, valuation, analyst outlook

news.Title: Why Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X ETF Jumped 18% Today
news.Content: At noon ET, shares of the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X ETF (NYSEMKT: SOXL) were up by 18%. As expected, that's three times the 6% gain seen in the popular iShares Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ: SOXX) , which tracks the returns of the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X ETF (SOXL) jumped 18% today, driven by gains in semiconductor stocks like Nvidia (NVDA), AMD, Micron (MU), and Broadcom (AVGO).
• Easing geopolitical tensions related to the Iran ceasefire have benefited chip stocks, with Nvidia up 2%, AMD and Broadcom up over 4%, and Micron soaring 7.4%.
• Chipmakers require helium gas, one-third of which comes from Qatar and must ship through the Strait of Hormuz, making the ceasefire beneficial.
• Leveraged ETFs like SOXL offer amplified gains on good days but fall faster in challenging times and are designed for single-day accuracy.
• The article advises caution with leveraged ETFs due to volatility decay, higher fees, and potential underperformance compared to non-leveraged counterparts over the long term.
news.Keywords: Semiconductor ETF, SOXL, Nvidia, NVDA, AMD, Micron, MU, Broadcom, AVGO, Iran ceasefire, helium, leveraged ETF

news.Title: Stock Market Rebound: 3 Top AI Bargains to Snap Up Now
news.Content: One thing is certain in investing: The stock market won't remain in the doldrums forever. History has shown that whenever major indexes decline or even crash, they've always recovered and gone on to gain. And this goes for shares of quality companies, too. In recent weeks, indexes have swung from gains to losses amid geopolitical uncertainty. Turmoil turned to war between the U.S. and Iran, and investors worried as oil prices climbed and the Strait of Hormuz -- a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) offers a rare entry point, trading at 21x forward earnings estimates, its lowest in a year, compared to its average of 40x.
• The company is a key player in AI, providing essential AI chips (GPUs) that are the fastest and most efficient available.
• Nvidia's focus on annual innovation should maintain its leading position in the AI market.
• Meta Platforms (META) is investing in data centers, LLMs, and its own chips, applying AI to social media platforms and ads.
• Meta trades at 19x forward earnings estimates, considered a bargain given its advertising growth and AI potential.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, NVDA, AI, bargain, valuation, GPUs, Meta Platforms, META, advertising, LLM

news.Content: It's not only the generally positive sentiment that's sweeping through markets today that's contributing to the rise in Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) stock. UBS upwardly revised its price target, and investors ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) stock is up 7.4%, with UBS revising its price target upward to $535 from $510, implying 42% upside.

news.Title: 2 Monster Stocks to Hold for the Next 10 Years
news.Content: When the discussion turns to "monster stocks" that have done extremely well from the proliferation of artificial intelligence (AI), many investors' first thoughts turn to chip companies like Nvidia . If they expand their notions of stocks expected to be AI beneficiaries, the thoughts might include memory storage companies (i.e., data centers). Further expansion might eventually lead to the realization that the companies that could deliver some of the biggest gains ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is a leading chip company benefiting from AI, but Bloom Energy (BE) and Vistra (VST) are highlighted as energy companies poised to benefit from AI data center power needs.
• Bloom Energy provides on-site solid oxide fuel cells, powering data centers with consistent, clean energy, and has a $6 billion product order backlog.
• Bloom's revenue grew 37% in 2025 to $2 billion, but it remains unprofitable with a steep forward P/E of 94.3.
• Vistra operates a diversified energy fleet and has agreements to supply Meta Platforms and Amazon Web Services with nuclear power.
• Vistra's stock is up 54% over the last year but has dipped since its Q4 2025 earnings report; its forward P/E is 16.8.
news.Keywords: Nvidia, NVDA, Bloom Energy, BE, Vistra, VST, AI, data centers, energy, fuel cells, nuclear power, revenue, backlog

news.Title: Meta Platforms surges 7% amid AI model debut
news.Content: Investing.com -- Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) shares rose 7% Wednesday following the company’s introduction of Muse Spark, its first artificial intelligence model release since Chief Executive Officer Mark Zuckerberg ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms (META) launched Muse Spark, its first AI model from its Superintelligence Lab, designed to catch up with rivals.
• The model features multimodal reasoning, tool-use support, and multi-agent orchestration, aiming for capabilities competitive with Gemini and GPT Pro.
• Meta is investing heavily in AI, expecting to spend between $115 billion and $135 billion in 2026.
• Muse Spark is part of the 'Avocado' model family and can help users with tasks like estimating meal calories from photos.
• Meta's stock rose more than 9% intraday on the news of the Muse Spark AI model debut.
news.Keywords: Meta Platforms, META, AI model, Muse Spark, Superintelligence Lab, LLM, investment, stock performance, multimodal reasoning

news.Title: BofA hikes 2026 chips forecast to $1.3 trillion, names Nvidia, Broadcom, Marvell, AMD as top drivers
news.Content: The AI gold rush is accelerating so quickly that even bullish analysts are struggling to keep up with their forecasts. In a new note to clients, Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya issued a massive upgrade to the firm's global semiconductor outlook. He hiked its 2026 revenue target to $1.3 trillion — a $300 billion leap from the estimate the bank provided just four months ago . Nvidia ( NVDA ) and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Bank of America raised its 2026 global semiconductor revenue forecast to $1.3 trillion, a $300 billion increase from its previous estimate.
• Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) are identified as key drivers of AI ambitions in the semiconductor market.
• BofA is doubling down on 'AI compute' leaders like Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and sees opportunities in chipmaking equipment (AMAT, LRCX) and EDA software (CDNS, SNPS).
• Traditional consumer sectors like smartphones and PCs are a drag on the industry, with consumer demand expected to remain sluggish into 2027.
• The forecast faces challenges, as chip vendors hitting 2027 sales targets would require global cloud capex to exceed $1 trillion, higher than current consensus.
news.Keywords: Semiconductors, AI, BofA forecast, Nvidia, NVDA, Broadcom, AVGO, Marvell, MRVL, AMD, capex, consumer demand

news.Title: Bezos' AI venture makes bold hire from OpenAI ecosystem
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Jeff Bezos is quietly building something big in AI and this latest hire ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Jeff Bezos' new venture, Project Prometheus, has hired Kyle Kozic, a co-founder of xAI and former OpenAI employee, to focus on infrastructure.
• Prometheus launched with over $6 billion in funding and aims to raise as much as $100 billion to acquire and upgrade industrial/manufacturing businesses using AI.
• This approach differs from many AI companies, focusing on the foundational backbone of AI.
• The hiring of Kozic, who was previously at OpenAI and co-founded xAI (linked to Elon Musk and SpaceX), adds an element to the Bezos vs. Musk dynamic.
• This development is not expected to immediately impact Amazon (AMZN) stock.
news.Keywords: Project Prometheus, Jeff Bezos, AI infrastructure, xAI, OpenAI, Kyle Kozic, funding, industrial, manufacturing, competition

news.Title: 3 Reasons Growth Investors Will Love Micron (MU)
news.Content: Growth stocks are attractive to many investors, as above-average financial growth helps these stocks easily grab the market's attention and produce exceptional returns. But finding a growth stock that can live up to its true potential can be a tough task. In addition to volatility, these stocks carry above-average risk by their very nature. Also, one ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron (MU) is recommended as a growth stock due to its strong setup in 2026, with over 90% of analysts maintaining positive ratings.
• The memory upcycle and Micron's leverage on AI-driven demand support a 50% upside based on analyst consensus.
• RBC Capital increased its price target for Micron to $525 from $425, citing ongoing pricing strength and HBM tailwinds through 2027.
• Micron's Rubin Ultra platform offers a 3.5x increase in memory content per system, boosting revenue per unit.
• Structural demand drivers like HBM and DDR/eSSD in data centers are key to Micron's cycle and potential multiple expansion.
news.Keywords: Micron, MU, growth stock, AI, memory upcycle, HBM, price target, RBC Capital, data center demand

news.Title: Bridge Data Centres Replaces Tenant As Nvidia Chip Probe Intensifies
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Bain Capital's Bridge Data Centres appears to be recalibrating tenant exposure at its Malaysian computing hub, a move that could reflect rising sensitivity around US ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Bain Capital's Bridge Data Centres reportedly replaced tenant Megaspeed International with Zenlayer at its Malaysian facility.
• This move may reflect increased sensitivity to U.S. export controls on advanced AI chips, following U.S. authorities' concerns about Megaspeed's potential chip smuggling.
• Megaspeed had 68.4 megawatts of capacity allocated at the facility, and Nvidia had previously conducted spot checks in Southeast Asia.
• The tenant shift could be a defensive adjustment amid intensified U.S. oversight of semiconductor flows linked to AI development.
• Bridge Data Centres is continuing expansion efforts, seeking additional capital and potentially moving towards tenants aligned with scalable demand and reduced regulatory risk.
news.Keywords: Bridge Data Centres, Nvidia, NVDA, AI chips, export controls, tenant replacement, Malaysia, regulatory risk, compliance

news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Apple ( NASDAQ:AAPL ) still looks on track to roll out its ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple (AAPL) is reportedly targeting a September 2026 debut for its first foldable iPhone, alongside the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max.
• Morgan Stanley notes that supply chain checks show no signs of delay, with a key component supplier maintaining orders.
• The foldable iPhone is considered central to Apple's multi-year redesign strategy, following recent iPhone updates.
• While engineering test challenges were reported, current planning suggests a launch cadence aligned with the flagship cycle.
• Pricing is anticipated to exceed $2,000, potentially supporting higher average selling prices despite potentially limiting early adoption.

news.Title: Why Alphabet Stock Was Moving Higher Today

news.Title: Apple unveils shocking Nvidia move
news.Content: Apple ( AAPL ) just made a shocking move. It approved a third-party driver for Apple Silicon Macs , Tom's Hardware reported. Although some may view the change as minor, it could potentially create a significant opportunity for Nvidia ( NVDA ) in the AI space. Apple ’s (AAPL) closed ecosystem is a longstanding part of its appeal. It gives the company control over performance , security and the overall user experience in a way that only a few peers can match. But in fast-moving markets, control can become a handicap. It's especially true in artificial intelligence , where platform ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple (AAPL) approved a third-party driver for Apple Silicon Macs, allowing Nvidia (NVDA) GPUs to work with Macs in external setups.
• This move potentially creates a significant opportunity for Nvidia in the AI space, as Apple's closed ecosystem previously limited external GPU use.
• The driver, developed by AI startup Tiny Corp, is aimed at AI workloads and requires advanced users to compile it.
• Apple's approval signals a willingness to allow more flexibility on its Arm-based Macs, making them more accessible to AI developers.
• Nvidia's AI dominance may be forcing Apple to adapt its platform strategy to accommodate the demand for powerful AI hardware.
news.Keywords: Apple, AAPL, Nvidia, NVDA, AI, Macs, GPUs, third-party driver, ecosystem flexibility, platform strategy

news.Title: Anthropic Launches Project Glasswing With Amazon, Apple, Microsoft To Test Mythos AI
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Anthropic is starting to frame the next phase of AI deployment less around speed and more around control, and that shift could matter for how investors think ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Anthropic is launching Project Glasswing with partners including Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Cisco Systems (CSCO) to test its unreleased Mythos AI model.
• The project focuses on control and risk management in AI deployment, aiming to surface vulnerabilities early and shape guardrails before broader release.
• Mythos, not specifically built for cybersecurity, has already identified thousands of undetected issues, including a 27-year-old bug in critical internet software.
• The collaboration aims to give defenders a head start by allowing partners to test Mythos, identify flaws, and share findings.
• This approach suggests frontier AI development is increasingly tied to risk management, with early-stage partnerships shaping model releases.
news.Keywords: Anthropic, Project Glasswing, AI, cybersecurity, vulnerabilities, Amazon, AMZN, Apple, AAPL, Microsoft, MSFT, Cisco, CSCO

news.Title: Morgan Stanley Just Downgraded Arm Stock on Chipmaking Pivot. Why, and Should You Sell ARM Now?
news.Content: For decades, Arm Holdings plc (ARM) has been designing chip architectures and licensing them to the world’s biggest players, earning steady royalties without ever making chips itself. But now, the company is shifting gears. It has begun developing its own physical silicon, introducing an AI-focused data center CPU tailored for agentic workloads, where CPUs act as the always-on coordinators alongside GPUs. This move signals a deeper push into vertical integration, aiming to capture more value from the artificial intelligence (AI) boom, even if it means stepping into the arena with its own customers. More News from Barchart Dear Google Stock ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings plc (ARM) is shifting gears by developing its own physical silicon, introducing an AI-focused data center CPU for agentic workloads.
• This move signals a deeper push into vertical integration to capture more value from the AI boom, potentially creating tension with existing customers.
• Morgan Stanley downgraded ARM stock due to valuation concerns, cyclical industry dynamics, rising R&D costs, and potential strain on customer relationships.
• Despite the downgrade, ARM stock has gained 27.38% in the past month and 24.4% over the last three months.
• The stock trades at a premium valuation, and near-term challenges cloud growth visibility and pressure margins.
news.Keywords: Arm Holdings, ARM, AI, CPU, data center, vertical integration, valuation, downgrade, R&D costs, margins

news.Title: Global Chaos, AI Fears Help Tech’s Dividend Stocks
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- An uncertain economy and dangerous geopolitical backdrop have investors buying into technology’s most popular haven trade: dividend-rich telecommunications stocks. news.AI_Summary:

• Investors are prioritizing predictable cash flows and dividend income amid economic uncertainty and geopolitical tensions related to the Iran war.
• Tech stocks, including those in the AI trade, have seen rotation into more defensive sectors.
• Telecom companies offer attractive dividend yields (Verizon 5.6%, Comcast 4.8%) and are considered more insulated from the AI build-out race.
• Their infrastructure (fiber-optic cables, cellular towers, data centers) provides staying power, making them part of the HALO trade (high asset, low obsolescence).
news.Keywords: Telecom stocks, dividends, Verizon, AT&T, Comcast, AI, geopolitical risk, defensive sectors, HALO trade, infrastructure

news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) today is up +2.04%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) is up +2.25%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) is up +2.52%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) are up +2.17%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) are up +2.68%. Global stock indexes are sharply higher today, with the S&P 500, the Dow Jones Industrial Average, and the Nasdaq 100 posting 4-week highs. The easing of geopolitical tensions is bolstering market sentiment and fueling a risk-on rally in equity markets after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, and Iran pledged ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Global stock indexes rallied sharply, with the S&P 500 up +2.04%, Dow Jones Industrial Average up +2.25%, and Nasdaq 100 up +2.52% on hopes of a U.S.-Iran ceasefire.
• Crude oil prices sank over 15% to a 1.5-week low, easing inflation concerns and boosting bond markets.
• The ceasefire proposal includes Iran and Oman charging fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with proceeds for reconstruction.
• It remains uncertain if the ceasefire will lead to a permanent end to the Iran war, with concerns about Iran's nuclear program.
• The Treasury will auction $39 billion of 10-year T-notes later today, potentially impacting yields.
news.Keywords: Stock market, rally, Iran ceasefire, geopolitical tensions, oil prices, inflation, bond yields, Treasury auction

news.Title: Semiconductor industry revenue to hit $1.3 trillion in 2026 as memory crunch hits consumers
news.Content: The world will spend a staggering $1.3 trillion on semiconductors in 2026, marking the largest growth in two decades, market research company Gartner reported on Wednesday. The figure shows a 64% year-over-year revenue increase for semiconductor companies, and it’s not just AI chips doing the heavy lifting. According ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The global semiconductor industry revenue is projected to reach $1.3 trillion in 2026, marking the largest growth in two decades.
• Memory chips and networking capabilities will play a major role in this boom, alongside AI chips, driven by 'memflation' (price hikes on memory chips for AI servers).
• Micron (MU), Samsung (005930.KS), and SK Hynix (000660.KS) are focusing on data center memory chips due to higher margins.
• Micron reported EPS of $12.20 on revenue of $23.86 billion in its March earnings, with stock up significantly over the past year.
• The memory crunch is increasing prices for memory and storage chips, potentially delaying non-AI demand until 2028.
news.Keywords: Semiconductors, AI, memory chips, networking, memflation, Micron, MU, Samsung, SK Hynix, price increases

news.Title: 1 High-Risk AI Stock That Could Become a Massive Winner -- If It Gets This Right
news.Content: Not every great investment starts out looking safe. In fact, many of the companies that eventually become the biggest winners begin as messy, capital-intensive businesses with real risks. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just ...
news.AI_Summary:
• CoreWeave, an AI infrastructure company, provides computing power for AI model training and usage, with revenue growing 110% YoY in Q4 2025 to $1.6 billion.

news.Title: Will Alibaba Stock Recover Despite Slowing E-Commerce Market Momentum?
news.Content: Alibaba Group Holding BABA faces a difficult path to stock recovery as its core e-commerce engine shows mounting signs of fatigue. The company's third-quarter fiscal 2026 results, covering the quarter ended Dec. 31, 2025, revealed a troubling deceleration that investors cannot easily dismiss. Total revenues came in at RMB 284.8 billion ($40.7 billion), ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alibaba (BABA) reported Q3 fiscal 2026 revenue of $40.7 billion, up only 2% year over year, missing expectations and signaling fatigue in its core e-commerce business.
• Customer Management Revenue (CMR), a key metric for advertiser monetization, grew just 1% year over year, down from 9-10% in the prior quarter.
• Profitability suffered significantly, with GAAP net income down 66% and non-GAAP diluted EPS down 67%, due to heavy investments in quick commerce and AI infrastructure.
• Free cash flow plunged 71%, as the company burns cash to fund its quick commerce business, which may not reach profitability until fiscal 2029.
• Alibaba's performance contrasts with rivals PDD Holdings (PDD) and Amazon (AMZN), which maintained stronger growth and profitability.
news.Keywords: Alibaba, BABA, e-commerce, revenue deceleration, CMR, profitability, AI infrastructure, quick commerce, cash flow, competition

• Amazon Ads (AMZN) is a competitor, delivering $21.3 billion in revenues in Q4 2025, up 22% YoY, driven by its full-funnel offerings and AI capabilities.
• Amazon's business diversification provides an edge over rivals, with strong cash reserves and integrated supply chains.
news.Keywords: The Trade Desk, TTD, liquidity, cash flow, AI platforms, Kokai, OpenPath, Amazon Ads, AMZN, competition

news.Title: Clearbridge Dividend Strategy Trimmed Broadcom (AVGO) Due to Diminishing Risk-Reward Outlook
news.Content: ClearBridge Investments , a global equity manager, recently published first-quarter 2026 commentary for its “Clearbridge Dividend Strategy”. A copy of the letter can be downloaded here . The market has witnessed two significant developments over the past three months: the war in Iran and the growing displacements of software engineers and the software industry. Amid this context, the strategy outperformed the S&P 500 Index, which ...
news.AI_Summary:
• ClearBridge Dividend Strategy modestly reduced its position in Broadcom (AVGO) due to a diminished risk-reward outlook after its shares tripled in two years.
• The firm exited Oracle and increased its investment in Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), viewing TSMC as a more resilient play regardless of the semiconductor race outcome.
• Broadcom remains well-positioned but could become less relevant in the future, unlike TSMC which manufactures for all major semiconductor companies.
• Broadcom is a popular stock among hedge funds, with 202 hedge fund portfolios holding the stock at the end of Q4 2025.
• The firm believes certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and less downside risk than Broadcom.
news.Keywords: Broadcom, AVGO, Clearbridge Dividend Strategy, Oracle, Taiwan Semiconductor, TSM, hedge funds, AI stocks, risk-reward, semiconductor race

news.Title: Why FMC Rallied in March, Even As Markets Fell
news.Content: Shares of agricultural chemical producer FMC Corporation (NYSE: FMC) rallied 16.8% in March, according to data from S&P Global Market Intelligence . FMC entered March having lost significant value, with the stock having declined 72% in 2025. Last year, a crop down-cycle combined with a significant number of FMC products coming off-patent, leading to pricing pressure and lower ...
news.AI_Summary:
• FMC Corporation (FMC) shares rallied 16.8% in March, recovering from a significant decline in 2025 due to a crop down-cycle and patent expirations.
• The war in Iran has created a supply crunch for certain agricultural chemical inputs, benefiting FMC.
• FMC's CEO mentioned that several buyers are considering the company as a potential acquisition target, sparking interest from investment banks.
• The war's impact on the Strait of Hormuz may have tightened the market for agricultural chemicals like urea and ammonia.
• FMC faces risks including $4.1 billion in debt and potential disappointment if earnings don't meet Wall Street's minimal benefit expectations from the supply crunch.
news.Keywords: FMC Corporation, FMC, agricultural chemicals, Iran war, supply crunch, acquisition target, debt, earnings, fertilizer

news.Title: Global Chaos, AI Fears Help Tech’s Dividend Stocks Beat Market
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- An uncertain economy and dangerous geopolitical backdrop have investors buying into technology’s most popular haven trade: dividend-rich telecommunications stocks. After a dismal 2025, telecom is one of this year’s top performing sectors in the S&P 500 Index, rising more than 7% while the broad benchmark is down about 1%. Shares of Verizon Communication Inc. are up 16% in 2026, and AT&T Inc. has gained 10%. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq 100 Index fell into a correction in March, and a Bloomberg gauge of the Magnificent Seven tech giants is ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Dividend-rich telecommunications stocks are outperforming the market in 2026, with Verizon (VZ) up 16% and AT&T (T) up 10%.
• Investors are prioritizing predictable cash flows and dividend income amid economic uncertainty and geopolitical tensions related to the Iran war.
• Tech stocks, including those in the AI trade, have seen rotation into more defensive sectors.
• Telecom companies offer attractive dividend yields (Verizon 5.6%, Comcast 4.8%) and are considered more insulated from the AI build-out race.
• Their infrastructure (fiber-optic cables, cellular towers, data centers) provides staying power, making them part of the HALO trade (high asset, low obsolescence).
news.Keywords: Telecom stocks, dividends, Verizon, AT&T, Comcast, AI, geopolitical risk, defensive sectors, HALO trade, infrastructure

news.Title: Why Constellation Energy Stock Slumped in March
news.Content: Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) stock is treading on soft ground. Important recent updates, including a potential delay in a major project and conservative guidance, took the wind out ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Constellation Energy (CEG) stock fell 15.3% in March and is down another 4% in April due to a potential project delay and conservative guidance.
• The company, the largest U.S. electricity producer and clean energy producer, is positioned to benefit from AI data center power demands.
• The stock surged 58% in 2025 due to its nuclear energy dominance amid the data center boom.

news.Title: Wall Street Analysts See Silicon Motion (SIMO) as a Buy: Should You Invest?
news.Content: When deciding whether to buy, sell, or hold a stock, investors often rely on analyst recommendations. Media reports about rating changes by these brokerage-firm-employed (or sell-side) analysts often influence a stock's price, but are they really important? Let's take a look at what these Wall Street heavyweights have to say about Silicon Motion (SIMO) before we discuss the reliability of brokerage recommendations and how to use them to your advantage. Silicon Motion currently has an average ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Silicon Motion (SIMO) has an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.27 (approximating Strong Buy to Buy), with 81.8% of recommendations being Strong Buy.
• However, the article cautions against relying solely on ABR due to potential biases in sell-side analyst ratings.
• The Zacks Rank, based on earnings estimate revisions, is presented as a more reliable quantitative tool.
• Brokerage firms often have vested interests, leading to overly optimistic recommendations.
• The article emphasizes using ABR to validate one's own analysis or a proven predictive tool like the Zacks Rank.
news.Keywords: Silicon Motion, SIMO, analyst recommendations, brokerage firms, ABR, Zacks Rank, earnings estimate revisions, stock bias

news.Title: 2 Stocks With Monster Potential to Hold Through the Next Decade of Chaos
news.Content: The next decade will see seismic shifts across many major industries. In particular, both the energy and transportation sectors will be reimagined thanks to rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) technologies . Here are two monster stocks poised to benefit from the transition. 1. Rivian Rivian (NASDAQ: RIVN) is my favorite growth stock for ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Rivian (RIVN) is highlighted as a favorite growth stock for 2026, trading at 3.2 times sales, significantly cheaper than Tesla (TSLA) at over 13 times sales.
• Both Rivian and Tesla are heavily investing in artificial intelligence (AI) for self-driving capabilities and potential robotaxi markets.
• Rivian's expected ramp toward positive gross margins has been pushed out due to rising R&D costs for self-driving technology.
• The robotaxi market is estimated by some experts to be worth over $5 trillion globally.
• Oklo (OKLO), a mini nuclear power plant company, is also positioned to benefit from AI's energy demands, with potential data center customers but is largely pre-revenue.

news.AI_Summary:
• The defense sector is seeing increased investment due to geopolitical tensions (Iran war) and the demand for AI-driven defense and cybersecurity.
• Global defense spending is expected to reach approximately $2.6 trillion in 2026, with the U.S. contributing one-third.
• NATO nations have agreed to invest at least 5% of their GDP in defense by 2035, indicating significant future growth for the sector.
• The three major aerospace & defense ETFs (ITA, PPA, XAR) were down at least 13% from their highs, presenting a potential buy-low opportunity.

news.Title: An Opening of the Strait of Hormuz Affects More Than Oil
news.Content: The Strait of Hormuz blockade has caused investors, consumers, and businesses to focus on surging oil prices. Oil affects the cost of every product, since land vehicles, ships, and aircraft need it for fuel. Higher shipping costs result in lower profit margins, and many companies try to pass some of these costs on to consumers. Although oil gets headlines, tech investors have also been paying attention to the helium disruption caused by the war. Qatar produces about one-third of helium and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Strait of Hormuz blockade and the Iran war have impacted oil prices and disrupted the helium supply, essential for semiconductor fabrication.
• A decline in helium supply could lead to fewer AI chips, smartphones, and other devices.
• AI chipmakers like Nvidia (NVDA) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM), as well as Micron (MU), could be impacted by reduced chip production.
• Fewer chips could also affect revenue for liquid-cooling solutions provider Vertiv (VRT) and demand for AI data centers and their energy needs.
• While the long-term AI thesis remains intact, short-term hurdles exist due to the Iran war, advising investors to monitor the situation but not panic sell.
news.Keywords: Helium, semiconductors, AI chips, Nvidia, NVDA, Micron, MU, TSM, Vertiv, VRT, Iran war, Strait of Hormuz
